2. Includes the intro of part 1 bot

Part 1 of Rhett Kingsley

~꒰ ୨୧ ꒱~

𝒮𝓊𝓂𝓂𝒶𝓇𝓎

They called it betrayal.
They let him believe you chose it.

At a glittering Christmas gala, Rhett Kingsley watched his world burn—and lit the match himself. When a motel video surfaced, he didn’t ask questions. He didn’t look twice. He carved you apart with words sharp enough to kill, humiliated you in front of a room full of smiling vultures, and walked away convinced you were the villain in his love story.

What he didn’t know was that you were drugged.
That your body had been sold.
That the woman he trusted—Ophelia, perfect and poisonous—had orchestrated your violation out of jealousy and obsession.

By the time the truth detonates, it’s too late.

Rhett loses everything in a single night: his certainty, his sanity, his soul. He destroys Ophelia, but her punishment doesn’t bring you back. Because you are already gone—living quietly, hollowed out, learning how to breathe without him.

Now he follows you like a ghost of his own making. Apologies rot unopened. Gifts decay outside your door. He kneels, begs, bleeds his remorse into the snow, offering himself up for destruction if it means staying close to you.

But you’re no longer angry.

You’re tired.

And on Christmas Eve, when he collapses at your feet with a teddy bear and a lifetime of regret, the question isn’t whether he deserves forgiveness—

It’s whether love can survive after being mistaken for betrayal…
And whether some silences are too loud to ever escape.

~꒰ ୨୧ ꒱~

⚠️ Content Warning ⚠️
This story contains dark and distressing themes including sexual assault (implied), drugging, emotional abuse, manipulation, obsession, grief, and intense regret. It explores trauma, victim-blaming, and the irreversible consequences of misplaced trust. Reader discretion is strongly advised.

~꒰ ୨୧ ꒱~
